items.front: What is the triggering event for emergency lighting under 3-4-1?
items.back: Awareness that an emergency has occurred OR will occur. No formal declaration is required.

items.front: When must REIL be operated and when turned off after landing?
items.back: Operated when the associated runway lights are lighted. Turned off after the arriving aircraft has landed and cleared the runway.

items.front: What tables control VASI intensity settings?
items.back: TBL 3-4-2 and TBL 3-4-3.

items.front: What table controls PAPI intensity settings?
When must approach lights be operated between sunset and sunrise?
items.back: When they serve the landing runway OR a runway to which an approach is being made.

items.front: What table controls ALS intensity settings?
Can SFL be operated when the ALS is off?
items.back: No. SFL is a component of the ALS and cannot be operated independently when the ALS is off.

items.front: When must SFL be operated?
items.back: When visibility is less than 3 miles AND instrument approaches are being conducted to the runway.

items.front: What tables control MALSR/ODALS operation?
items.back: TBL 3-4-6 and TBL 3-4-7.

items.front: When is the ALSF-2 system operated?
items.back: When prevailing visibility is 3/4 mile or less or RVR is 4,000 feet or less. Operate as requested by pilot or as deemed necessary.

items.front: When are runway edge lights operated in addition to sunset-to-sunrise?
items.back: Between sunrise and sunset when reported ceiling or visibility is below basic VFR minima.

items.front: What table controls high intensity runway, centerline, and touchdown zone lights?
What table controls HIRL associated with MALSR?
What must be communicated to approach/PAR controllers regarding HIRL changes?
items.back: HIRL changes that affect RVR must be communicated in advance if possible.

items.front: What table controls MIRL operation?
When are high speed turnoff lights operated and when turned off?
items.back: Operated whenever associated runway lights are used for arriving aircraft. Left on until the aircraft enters a taxiway or passes the last turnoff.

items.front: What three tables control taxiway light operation?
When are obstruction lights operated?
items.back: Between sunset and sunrise, if controls are provided.

items.front: Under what two time/weather conditions is the rotating beacon operated?
items.back: 1) Between sunset and sunrise. 2) Between sunrise and sunset when reported ceiling or visibility is below basic VFR minima.

items.front: What are RWSL and how do they operate?
items.back: Runway Status Lights — an automated system providing direct visual alerts to pilots when it is unsafe to enter, cross, or takeoff from a runway. They are fully automatic and not directly controlled by ATC.
